decision-tree.root-question: Which orchestrator shape fits this task profile (length, budget cap, safety veto, hand-off)?

branch.conclusion: Skip this methodology. Use a direct SDK call or pretty-print mode; the parsing overhead exceeds value.

branch.when: task.expected_duration_seconds >= 30 and budget_cap_usd is not None
branch.conclusion: Full Python `StreamHandler` class with rolling cost tracker; budget cap triggers `proc.kill()`.
Event-dispatcher pattern with veto rule set; inspect every `tool_use` before execution.
Detect hand-off signal in assistant text (regex against parsed content), kill current proc, spawn next with the carried context.
Per-event OTel span mapping; persist the JSONL replay file as the source of truth for traces.
Skip this methodology. Use the CLI's pretty-print mode directly with the user; orchestration is not the right tool.
Skip this methodology and surface a defect against the CLI; do not roll your own JSONL emitter on top of pretty-print.

example.p: Discord bot agent task: duration ≥30s, budget_cap_usd=0.50 → branch matches `budget_cap` → conclusion `r6-budget-veto` → instantiate `StreamHandler(allowed_tools=..., max_turns=20, budget_cap_usd=0.50)`.
